witnesses.description: Every element in a three-element universe satisfies P.
A finite predicate with exactly one listed true witness.
A finite predicate where an existential statement holds but a universal statement fails.
A binary predicate table with R(a, b) true and R(b, a) false.

checks.claim: The listed finite predicate table satisfies forall x. P(x).
checks.notes: The validator checks every universe element has predicate value true.

checks.claim: The listed finite predicate table satisfies exists x. P(x).
checks.notes: The validator checks the listed witness element is in the universe and has predicate value true.

checks.claim: There exists a unary predicate over the non-empty finite universe such that forall x. P(x) holds and exists x. P(x) fails.

data.certificate: The regression parses the source-linked finite quantifier-expansion DIMACS artifact, emits a DRAT proof, elaborates it to LRAT, and independently checks both proof objects against the CNF.

checks.notes: The validator enumerates every unary predicate valuation over the non-empty finite universe and finds no counterexample; the cargo regression checks the emitted DRAT/LRAT proof route for the matching DIMACS artifact.

checks.claim: There is a finite predicate table where exists x. P(x) holds but forall x. P(x) fails.
checks.notes: The validator checks the true witness and the false counterexample element.

checks.claim: There is a finite binary predicate table that violates symmetry.
checks.notes: The validator checks R(a, b) is present and R(b, a) is absent.

checks.claim: General first-order validity over arbitrary domains requires a future proof-assistant resource.

data.target_theorem_shape: first-order semantic validity and completeness over arbitrary domains
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite rows are checked; arbitrary-domain first-order validity remains a proof-assistant horizon.
